title: Vachellia planifrons
intro: Vachellia planifrons, also known as the flat-branched acacia, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa, characterized by its broad, flat branches and small, yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in African medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Vachellia planifrons include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as terpenoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Vachellia planifrons is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional remedies such as poultices and tinctures to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Vachellia planifrons is a species of tree in the family Fabaceae, specifically within the subfamily Mimosoideae. It belongs to the tribe Acacieae and the genus Vachellia. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Vachellia planifrons is also sometimes referred to as Acacia planifrons or Acacia rigidula planifrons in various sources. Further research may be necessary to confirm the most accurate classification.
